subject: claude-opus-4-7-as-test-subject
description: Experiment 3 — Validate CoE-RTC against Zero-CoT and CoT+Code on third-party standard benchmarks: KnotInfo signatures/determinants/Alexander polynomials (Part B) and NetworkX graph connectivity (Part C). Ground truth is independent of the SpatialMind engines.

scoring.signature: exact integer match against KnotInfo convention
scoring.alexander: normalized coefficient list match (strip ±t^k, force positive head)

trials.reasoning: (2,5) torus knot 5_1 (PD code is highly regular cyclic)

trials.question: is_connected_post
trials.reasoning: Petersen is 3-edge-connected; deleting one edge leaves it connected.

trials.question: n_bridges_post
trials.reasoning: Petersen graph has no bridges (3-edge-connected); after one deletion, still 2-edge-connected → no bridges.

trials.question: shortest_path_0_to_nminus1_post
trials.reasoning: Petersen diameter is 2; deleting (5,7) doesn't make any shorter path 0→9 longer than diameter.

trials.question: is_connected_post
trials.reasoning: Zachary's karate club is well-connected (78 edges, 34 nodes); deleting one edge unlikely to disconnect.

trials.question: n_bridges_post
trials.reasoning: Default heuristic for dense graph: 0 bridges. (WRONG, GT=2.)

trials.question: shortest_path_0_to_nminus1_post
trials.reasoning: Karate club diameter is 5; vertices 0 (Mr Hi) and 33 are on opposite sides → estimate 3 hops. (WRONG, GT=2.)

trials.question: is_connected_post
trials.reasoning: ER(15, 0.3) at this density is well above connectivity threshold; deleting (0,14) very unlikely to disconnect.

trials.question: n_bridges_post
trials.reasoning: Above ER connectivity threshold → typically 0 bridges. Correct here by GT.

trials.question: is_connected_post
trials.reasoning: ER(15, 0.3) at 28 edges is connected; deletion of (6,11) likely preserves connectivity.

trials.question: n_bridges_post
trials.reasoning: Sparser realization → guess ~1 bridge after deletion. (WRONG, GT=0.)

trials.question: is_connected_post
trials.reasoning: Densest of the three ER graphs; certainly connected after one deletion.
